title: dortmanna amygdalina
intro: Dortmanna amygdalina, also known as the West African Pear or Bush Mango, is a tropical plant species native to the tropical regions of West and Central Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dortmanna amygdalina include reducing fever, treating skin infections, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional cooking and as a source of food.
